Data_Source: NABARD Micro-Irrigation Survey 2023 + MoA Crop Calendar
Justification: Kharif accounts for 46% of annual drip pipe demand in Maharashtra (sugarcane + cotton sowing)
Impact_on_Price: +28% demand vs annual average -> pushes AI price up

Justification: Rabi onion/tomato cultivation drives second demand peak

Data_Source: Historical distributor sales data (Jain Irrigation market report)
Justification: Off-season: heat prevents field installation; farmers wait for Kharif
Impact_on_Price: −20% demand vs annual average -> AI prices conservatively

Data_Source: PM-KUSUM scheme annual report 2023 + Ministry of Agriculture drip expansion targets
Justification: National drip irrigation area growing at 9-11% CAGR (2018-2023)

Data_Source: NCPAH 2023: Gujarat has 7.2 lakh ha under micro-irrigation (highest in India)
Justification: Largest micro-irrigation state; developed dealer network and subsidy penetration drives premium pricing

Data_Source: NABARD Bihar State Focus Paper 2023: 0.5 lakh ha under drip
Justification: Lowest adoption — price must be kept competitive to penetrate market

Data_Source: Calibrated against price elasticity of B2B agri-input markets
Justification: If demand doubles (2x average): price rises only 25%. B2B buyers switch supplier if price spikes. Keeps us competitive.
Impact_on_Price: Controls how strongly ML demand shifts the price

Data_Source: Industry practice for B2B pricing stability (reference: McKinsey B2B Pricing 2022)
Justification: 55% weight on stable base price prevents daily fluctuations that erode distributor trust. 45% AI signal is sufficient for responsiveness.
Impact_on_Price: Limits max price swing to ~±18% from base

Data_Source: Calibrated: max Gujarat premium should not exceed 3% (above logistics) to stay price-competitive
Justification: Adoption affects demand volume not manufacturing cost — so impact is capped small vs logistics which has direct cost basis.

Data_Source: Jain Irrigation and Netafim dealer pricing benchmarks (Maharashtra market survey)
Justification: When demand is strong -> capture more margin toward competitor ceiling. 0.55 of the price-gap is captured.
Impact_on_Price: Adds up to +5% to price when demand is high

Justification: When demand is weak -> undercut by 20% of the price gap to stimulate sales.
Impact_on_Price: Reduces price by up to −3% when demand is below average

Data_Source: NIC Logistics Ease Across Different States (LEADS) Report 2023
Justification: Assam is 2600km by road. LTL freight from Pune at ₹28/km/MT = ₹12.3% transport surcharge.
